result:
Done — `python3 -m pytest -q` passes with all 6 tests green. Two bugs were fixed in `lrucache/cache.py`, both causing valid entries to be wrongly evicted:

1. **`get` didn't refresh recency** (`cache.py:30`): a cache hit returned the value without moving the key to the most-recently-used end of the `OrderedDict`, so eviction order ignored reads. Added `move_to_end(key)` on a non-expired hit.

2. **`put` evicted on in-place updates** (`cache.py:33`): the eviction check fired whenever the cache was at capacity, even when the key being written already existed — so updating a key in a full cache evicted an unrelated entry. Now it only evicts when the key is new.

`SOLUTION.md` at the repo root documents both root causes.
